text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little town nestled among rolling hills and oak trees stood a garden famous across the land for its magical flowers that sang when you walked near them. There was one particular flower, a brilliant flower with a face like a human girl, who everyone had heard singing the sweetest song.

Every autumn, the townsfolk would gather in Sweet Hollow to listen to the flower's gentle lullaby, enchanted by its melody. This happened one crisp morning, and a young girl named Lily wandered into the garden. She loved visiting the flowerbeds because they always seemed to smile at her, even when she thought them silent.

That particular autumn day, the flower didn't sing. It smiled warmly at Lily instead. Intrigued, Lily sat close, listening. The smile didn't move – only changed slightly – as the flower spoke.

"You're so sweet to come here," it sang instead of singing.
